rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND VS. INVESTCO MANAGEMENT & DEVELOPMENT LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively For Summary Adjudication, By Plaintiff Under Civil Code Section 437c
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively For Summary Adjudication, By Plaintiff Under Civil Code Section 437c. Plaintiff The People of the State of California, by and through the Commissioner of Business Oversight's motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. Defendant Douglas R. Hanson does not dispute that the court had the authority to amend the duties of the Special Master and the interlocutory judgment and to issue the stay on the litigation against the LLCs. Moreover, the court has inherent power to amend existing orders and to issue new orders where, as here, it has jurisdiction. (Gold v. Gold (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 791, 806-07.) Defendant delayed in opposing the Amended Orders while complying with those orders and accepting the benefits of being relieved from paying the Special Master's fees. It is now too late to question the Orders' validity as to the guarantee provisions of the Settlement Agreement. Defendant's assertions about lack of knowledge of the Amended Orders and their effects are not supported by the evidence before the court. The failure of Hanson to read the emails and the attachments from his attorney is not dispositive in light of Hanson's own testimony and that of attorney Marc Haber. More importantly, this ignorance does not excuse Hanson's choice to comply with the Amended Orders sought by Haber and reap their benefits but try to avoid their other obligations. "[Defendants] must reckon with the elementary rule of agency law that a principal is not allowed to ratify the unauthorized acts of an agent to the extent that they are beneficial, and disavow them to the extent that they are damaging. If a principal ratifies part of a transaction, he is deemed to ratify the whole of it." (Navrides v. Zurich Ins. Co. (1971) 5 Cal.3d 698, 704; see also Civil Code §1589 ["A voluntary acceptance of the benefit of a transaction is equivalent to a consent to all the obligations arising from it, so far as the facts are known, or ought to be known, to the person accepting."].) Finally, Defendants have not appealed the Amended Orders and they cannot do so now as the 180-day window for appeal has long since expired and Amended Orders are now res judicata. (CCP §904.1; CRC §8.104; Pacific Mut. Life Ins. Co. of Cal. v. McConnell (1955) 44 Cal.2d 715, 725.) Plaintiff is therefore entitled to judgment as a matter of law on its claim for declaratory relief of the rights and obligations of the parties under the 2012 Settlement Agreement and the 2014 Amended Orders. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MPL BRANDS, INC. VS. BRONCO WINE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 2, RESPONDENT BRONCO WINE COMPANY's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Respondent Bronco Wine Co.'s motion to change venue of this petition for arbitration is DENIED. Venue for an arbitration petition is governed by CCP §1292, which authorizes venue in a county where the agreement is to be performed. Petitioner MPI Brands, Inc. shows that Bronco was to make payments under the agreement in San Francisco and in fact made some such payments. (Patane Dec. 5, Ex. A; Anaheim Extrusion Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 1201.) No sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EDDIE SUMMERFIELD VS. GUIZHEN LUO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Right To Attach And An Order Attaching Claimants Lien Pursuant To Directions In Courts 10 25 2018 Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 3, CLAIMANT ADIL HIRAMANEK's Motion For Right To Attach And An Order Attaching Claimants Lien Pursuant To Directions In Courts 10 25 2018 Order. Claimant Hiramanek's motion for right to attach is DENIED. Hiramanek fails to demonstrate the probable validity of his claim per CCP 481.190. Summerfield denies ever signing the promissory note and Hiramanek does not show that it is more likely than not that his claim has merit. (Summerfield Dec., 1.) rulings.Case Title: HAR KWAN LUK DBA NANCY WONG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS ET AL VS. TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration And Revocation Of Order Granting Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF HAR KWAN LUK AKA NANCY WONG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS, KWOK CHUNG WONG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS, STEPHEN WONG, LISA WONG's Motion For Reconsideration And Revocation Of Order Granting Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Plaintiffs' motion for reconsideration and revocation of order granting motion to set aside default and default judgment is DENIED. Plaintiffs lacked reasonable diligence in adducing their "new evidence," which was in any event duplicative of an insurer's declination they apparently already knew about. Further, a lawyer's knowledge is not necessarily imputable to a client, particularly when he might not represent the client in a new matter. (See Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 738.) California's liberal policy is to decide lawsuits on their merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is GRANTED. It appears amendment may foreclose time-consuming disputes over the complaint's verbiage, and "trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceedings." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) All requests for trial continuances must be directed to Department 206. rulings.Case Title: ERIC B. WALL DDS VS. THE CANADA LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Canada Life Assurance Company, Computer Sciences Corporation, Swiss Re Life & Health America, Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 17, Defendant The Canada Life Assurance Company, Computer Sciences Corporation, Swiss Re Life & Health America, Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only). Defendants Computer Sciences Corporation and Swiss Re Life & Health America, Inc.'s motions for summary judgment (or adjudication on issues 4-11) are granted. Plaintiff admits that these entities are not parties to the disability policy at undisputed facts 3 and 4. This admission shows that there is no basis to sue them. In opposition, plaintiff's adduced evidence does not show that they are "Managing General Agents" that are liable for Canada Life's conduct per Insurance Code § 769.81. (See McLeod Depo., 7:4-14; 17:13-18:11; 29:22-24; Millar Depo., 10:9-11:22.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC B. WALL DDS VS. THE CANADA LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant The Canada Life Assurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Defendants Computer Sciences Corporation And Swiss Re Life And Health America, Incs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANT THE CANADA LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY, COMPUTER SCIENCES CORPORATION, SWISS RE LIFE & HEALTH AMERICA, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues.(part 1 of 2) Defendant Canada Life Assurance Company's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, and 3. There are triable issues of material fact regarding whether Canada Life breached the contract, breached the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and whether plaintiff is entitled to punitive damages. (See Fletcher v. Western National Life Ins. Co. (1970) 10 Cal.App.3d 376.) "It is well established that a breach of the implied covenant of good faith is a breach of the contract¿" (Schwartz v. State Farm Fire and Cas. Co. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 1329, 1339.) "[E]ven an insurer that pays the full limits of its policy may be liable for breach of the implied covenant, if improper claims handling causes detriment to the insured." (Id.) In addition, the insurer's unreasonable classification of a disability as caused by "sickness" rather than "injury" (so as to deny or limit benefits) may breach its implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing with the insured. In this case, plaintiff presents substantial evidence that the accident "triggered" the injury, he is entitled to lifetime benefits, and Canada Life ignored this information in an attempt to limit plaintiff's benefits. (Opgennorth Dec., Ex. 3, 839, 1155, 1464, 2603, 2855, 2862, 5293, 3142, 5719-5721, 4566-4569. Claims manager McLeod admitted that prior to the boating accident, plaintiff did not have functional limitations. (Opgennorth Dec., Ex. 4, McLeod Depo., 160:3-22.) McLeod further admitted that Canada Life did not speak with plaintiff's physicians and it learned that Mass Mutual treated the disability as a result of an accident. (Id. at 57:5-7; 90:3-91:14.) Dr. Morton's testimony also suggests that the boating accident caused the disability. (Opgennorth Dec., Ex. 5, Morton Depo., 39:4-10; 47:18-24; 71:23-72:5.) The federal action can be further evidence of bad faith given this record. "It is at least arguable that pursuing a declaratory relief action regarding coverage could be done for reasons indicating bad faith may be present: e.g., if there were no proper cause to dispute coverage, and if more than an erroneous interpretation of a policy (e.g., a willfully misguided one) were concerned." (Dalrymple v. USAA (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 497, 515.) Plaintiff incurred financial loss by having to defend the alleged bad faith federal action. Bad faith damages exist whether an insured hires an attorney to obtain benefits due or defend benefits that an insurer is attempting to wrongfully remove or withhold.(end of part 1 of 2) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. SAXON WOO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2019, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF CROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Defendant Woo's claim of exemption is DENIED. $2,425 of Woo's money is exempt under CCP 704.080(b)(2). Woo fails to cite a basis to exempt the excess of $717.35. Woo appears to argue that the excess is necessary for his support. Woo fails to cite a statutory basis to exempt the excess and fails to meet his burden under CCP 704.080(e)(4). If Woo is relying on the necessary for support exemption of CCP 706.051(b), that exemption does not apply as this case does not involve an earnings withholding order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
